text:
s of the weekdays
are said to be Lion for Friday, Tiger for Monday, Elephant,
Donkey, Boar, Cow, and Dog for the rest. In addition to this
there is Varasula—weekday Spike— which should be avoided for
journeys in the particular direction: There is ^iiia on Monday and
Saturday towards the East; on Thursday towards the South; on
Sunday and Friday towards the West, and on Tuesday and
Wednesday towards the North. However, there are some excep-
tions to this rule during emergencies: Monday and Saturday Sula
will not do any harm after Eight Ghatis have elapsed; on Tuesday
and Wednesday after Twelve Ghatis; on Sunday and Friday after
Fifteen Ghatis; and on Thursday after Twenty-two Ghatis. As
faras Sraddhas are concerned. Monday and Saturday are the best;
Tuesday and Friday are the worst; and the rest tolerable.
The third limb of the Almanac is the Naksatra or constellation
whose importance must needs be specially noted by ail in general
and by students of astrology in particular.
